change_log_latest:
• 🟢 AI Studio Backend Plan Mode is now a durable production lane with managed Production Director startup, systemd-friendly service ownership, and update-safe backend execution.
• ⚙️ Backend Plan Mode now persists finished chats, completion summaries, generated media, and final conversation state more reliably so proof conversations survive chat switches, reloads, and browser-save hiccups.
• ⚙️ Backend Plan and Interactive video prompting now preserve literal single-shot requests, add sequencing structure only when asked, and sanitize prompts to avoid unwanted props, text, markings, or story embellishments.
• ⚙️ Backend Plan Mode now routes automatically against installed AI Studio assets, falls back cleanly when keyframe models are missing, and keeps visual-only renders as silent videos instead of failed jobs.
• 🐞 AI Studio Director restarts now use the correct control-owned healthcheck against the real `/v1/models` endpoint, and Director support assets now count as installed readiness instead of appearing as missing downloads.
• ⚙️ Model Scores now uses resumable stage-level evidence, authoritative artifact classification, queue-order preservation, and targeted missing-stage reruns so interrupted or partial runs can be repaired without starting over.
• 🖥️ Benchmarks UI now shows richer per-stage evidence colors and icons, mobile-safe log wrapping, stable live log headers, and in-place stage updates without full modal rebuilds.
• ⚡ Benchmark inventory refreshes now use persisted caches, bounded log parsing, compact polling, and explicit evidence-repair paths so large score histories no longer freeze the Benchmarks modal.
• 🐞 Control-plane updates now bind admin and proxy listeners before background warmups and return lightweight warming snapshots, eliminating recurring readiness warnings and early 502s during updates.
• 🖥️ File Editor now exposes inline Delete beside Discard Changes and reuses the Storage Browser confirmation flow while keeping delete disabled in preview and read-only mode.
• 🐞 Fixed web UI model downloads so the control service resolves user-local Hugging Face CLI installs such as uv tool's ~/.local/bin/hf.
change_log_release:
v0.10.0

• 🧩 Migration to Club-3090 v0.10 now normalizes custom compose mounts, restores benchmark score backups, relinks OLD score history, and preserves selected-stage repair chains so upgraded systems keep their historical benchmarking state.
• 🟢 Chat gained a separate Backend Plan Mode target powered by the upstream AI Studio Production Director, while AI Studio setup and start can launch that backend planning service directly from the panel.
• ⚡ AI Studio Director now uses dynamic GPU-first auto placement with CPU fallback, and text-only Plan or Interactive steps prefer an already-loaded chat runtime before waking the Director.
• ⚙️ Plan and Interactive no-media flows now stay genuinely text-only, honor worded step counts, and use the canonical Director path end-to-end when fallback text generation is needed.
• 🧰 Model Scores refreshes now rely on compact live snapshots, shared score caches, and lighter inventory payloads so active benchmark sessions stay responsive instead of blocking on full score rescans.
• ⚙️ Model Scores now tracks missing-stage reruns, Fast versus Turbo evidence, bounded reasoning budgets, vLLM-Omni text-only adaptation, thermal waits, stale-run cleanup, and preserved boot-failure logs more coherently.
• 🖥️ The Benchmarks modal now opens against fresher inventory state, keeps child and parent selection checkboxes aligned, and preserves accurate eligible-row grouping during active scoring sessions.
• 🧩 Compatibility work now keeps deprecated DFlash and Turbo presets running on preserved local images instead of dead nightly tags, while v0.10.1 profile imports keep canonical preset slugs and image pins intact.
• 🐞 Installer, update, and startup flows now preflight Docker image headroom before builds or pulls, reducing failed updates on low-space systems and giving clearer readiness behavior after restarts.
• 🐞 Control startup now binds the admin and proxy listeners before warmups and returns lightweight warming snapshots immediately, so the panel comes back faster after updates instead of hanging on cold status rebuilds.
• 🧩 AI Studio inventory and Model Manager reporting now recover migrated local Studio resources, storage usage, and readiness state so v0.10 installs surface the right downloaded-lane picture right away.

v0.9.44

• 🟢 AI Studio added the upstream Z-Image, Krea 2, 10Eros, Wan2.2, and stronger Step-Audio lanes across setup, downloads, readiness checks, Resource Manager, and Plan or Interactive routing.
• 🧩 AI Studio was rebased onto the unified upstream `ai-studio` scene with canonical `/admin/ai-studio` routes, current labels, Chroma and HiDream-O1 gap-filling, and CPU-default Director behavior that stays compatible without patching upstream files.
• 🖥️ AI Studio resource detection and lane cards now understand the expanded lane roster, show Download Missing when shared assets are incomplete, and classify the new Studio assets into the right modality groups.
• ⚙️ AI Studio prompt routing now respects the exact requested lane more faithfully, including Step-Audio versus Kokoro selection and the new image or video lanes, instead of collapsing requests back to older defaults.
• ⚙️ AI Studio video generation now supports upstream-style Wan2.2 chained long clips and 720p DisTorch rendering while also improving prompt quality for LTX, Sulphur, and Wan outputs.
• 🧩 Migration now preserves shared-compose aliases, custom mounts, support directories, model trees, cache trees, and checkout-relative AI Studio paths inside control-owned storage so preserved presets no longer depend on old checkouts.
• 🧰 Migration now also vendors smaller support-file mounts into control-owned preset storage and moves preserved model, cache, AI Studio, result, and LMCache trees before symlinking, which reduces peak disk duplication during upgrades.
• 🖥️ Admin panel reliability improved with persistent session cookies, a cached offline shell, last-known status fallback, calmer update-mode handoff, and lighter live polling for status and Metrics.
• ⚙️ Benchmarks scheduling now handles exclusive-slot waits, thermal retries, GPU1 device visibility, queue ordering, failed launch rows, and completed-session review much more predictably.
• ⚙️ Benchmark thermal safety now uses clearer long-window limits plus critical overheat trips, targeted safe-power fallback, normalized saved messages, and more informative collapsed GPU telemetry.
• 🐞 Benchmark results now prune superseded stale runs and cross-mode sidecars so score badges, session review, and selected-stage repairs reflect only the current valid Quick and Full artifacts.

v0.9.43

• ⚙️ Plan and Interactive Mode now repair Director media plans into deterministic speech, image, and video batches that preserve authoritative source fields and synthesize missing requested lanes when the Director leaves them out.
• ⚙️ Plan and Interactive execution now records richer Executor notes, progress states, attachments, outputs, timestamps, and generation durations directly into saved chat conversations.
• ⚙️ Plan and Interactive source-data generation now validates exact item counts and script word-count requirements, repairs short or long outputs deterministically, and keeps real-world factual prompts conservative instead of hallucination-prone.
• ⚡ AI Studio video prompting now expands rough requests into structured LTX and Sulphur scenes with explicit no-text guidance, duration-aware rendering, and cleaner one-output routing for voice, music, SFX, image, and video steps.
• ⚙️ Ideogram and media routing now honor true single-image requests, produce stronger deliberate variation when multi-candidate grids are intended, and send the correct prompt payloads to Kokoro, Step-Audio, image, and video lanes.
• 🧰 AI Studio setup, start, and removal now automate baseline symlink repair, workflow-preview checks, Hugging Face Xet disablement, and queue preview wiring so ComfyUI jobs stay inspectable and easier to recover.
• 🧰 AI Studio generation now automatically wakes the correct power profile, drives fans up before heavy jobs, and keeps ComfyUI workflow metadata previewable from both queued and running jobs.
• 🐞 Saved AI Studio and chat-generated media now normalize permissions and timestamp formats better, so old and new conversations can reopen embedded artifacts more reliably.
• ⚙️ Model Scores reruns now stay scoped to the requested presets and stages, repair failed saved scores only when the failing gate actually clears, and refuse to let incomplete selected-stage artifacts masquerade as full scores.
• ⚙️ Model Scores thermal handling now defers and retries hot stages more intelligently, pauses active runtimes during cooldown, applies safer power caps after thermal events, and keeps GPU reservations until cleanup settles.
• 🐞 GPU-scoped benchmark launches, stale runtime relaunches, and live Benchmarks refreshes were hardened so running queues advance reliably without false endpoint failures, stale queue resumes, or bad GPU-index mapping.
• 🖥️ Plan and Interactive progress UX now shows clearer asset-generation state, better yellow status text, cleaner audio embedding, and more legible per-lane execution summaries in the transcript.

v0.9.42

• ⚙️ Plan Mode execution now forwards dependent media outputs into later video and voice steps and keeps richer chain context across the whole AI Studio plan, making multi-step requests compose correctly instead of losing intermediate artifacts.

v0.9.41

• 🟢 AI Studio expanded into a real generation surface with direct chat lanes for image, audio, video, and voice work, including progress, cancellation, transcript embeds, and File Editor preview access.
• 🟢 Chat AI Studio now supports LTX and Sulphur video, image-to-video attachments, Step-Audio voice cloning, Kokoro voiceover, and Director-backed planning as selectable generation lanes.
• 🖥️ AI Studio gained a dedicated menu entry, modality-grouped lane cards, responsive lane columns, richer docs/help affordances, visible runtime status, and a browsable Gallery for recent outputs.
• 🧩 AI Studio outputs are now persisted as durable chat-owned artifacts, so saved conversations can reload embedded image, audio, and video results instead of losing locally generated media context.
• 🧰 AI Studio setup and startup now use repo-local model storage, private Hugging Face CLI provisioning, persisted credentials, direct ComfyUI orchestration, optional sidecar services, and richer per-lane download bundles.
• 🧰 AI Studio setup also shifted optional services like LiteLLM, SearXNG, and Open WebUI into a more clearly optional role while keeping ComfyUI as the primary rendering backend.
• ⚙️ AI Studio lane readiness now requires the correct complete model sets and shared dependencies before enabling a lane, including Director, Step-Audio, LTX/Sulphur, Chroma, Ideogram, and Gemma support assets.
• ⚙️ AI Studio runtime management now handles GPU handoffs between renderer and voice services, keeps same-kind caches hot, wakes the right power profile when jobs start, and updates activity counters immediately.
• 🟢 The AI Studio Director became a selectable chat target, allowing backend-directed planning and routing from within the normal conversation surface.
• 🖥️ Model Manager and Status now treat AI Studio as a first-class runtime with service state, queued activity, modality-classified resources, and lane-specific download or delete actions.
• ⚡ High-throughput chat streaming now uses adaptive reveal pacing and visible buffering so very fast models paint steadily instead of in jagged bursts.
• 🐞 Page refresh, tab restore, scroll restore, update-mode handoff, and benchmark-versus-AI-Studio mutual exclusion were all hardened so the panel stays in the right place and GPU workloads do not overlap accidentally.

v0.9.40

• 🟢 AI Studio became a first-class Presets entry with setup actions, lane cards, modality-aware Model Manager classification, and a dedicated panel surface instead of being a side workflow.
• 🟢 Chat gained direct audio and video attachment handling with distinct previews and forwarding to compatible multimodal runtimes.
• 🧰 Setup Image Studio can now be launched from the panel with streamed logs and setup-driven download progress for lanes such as Gemma 12B QAT.
• ⚡ High-throughput chat streaming now repaints very fast model output smoothly instead of landing in giant transcript bursts.
• 🖥️ File Editor tabs now expose full paths on hover and allow downloading the active file even in read-only preview mode.

v0.9.39

• 🧩 Migration now preserves custom and historical presets more faithfully, only adds `-OLD` when there is a real upstream collision, restores deprecated and NVLink variants, and carries historical scores and TPS samples onto preserved rows.
• 🖥️ Presets gained rich filtering and sorting across badges, score ranges, score categories, engine, topology, benchmark TPS, runtime status, and migration provenance, with live match counts and one-click reset.
• 🖥️ OLD preset rows now stay grouped beside their current counterparts with clearer migrated, custom, and NVLink badge treatment, plus more consistent category placement and ordering.
• 🧰 Model Manager expanded into a real resource manager with accurate model-versus-cache accounting, safe cleanup previews, exact resource ownership, direct preset jumps, and persistent resource marker colors.
• 🧰 Model Manager now distinguishes model payloads from runtime or precompile cache, lists unattached resources safely, and gives clearer per-resource delete confirmation for shared files.
• ⚡ Model downloads and setup-backed installs now start faster, stream progress sooner, retry transient Hugging Face failures, preserve partial downloads, and attach setup-installed assets and drafters correctly.
• 🧰 GPU-scoped launches now map host GPUs to container-local ordinals correctly across Docker, vLLM, and llama.cpp-style presets, fixing GPU1 and per-scope runtime reliability.
• 🟢 Added AI Studio to the panel and chat with setup/start flows, modality-aware lane cards, resource management, image/audio/video generation, attachments, previews, and initial Studio model download handling.
• 🧩 Migration and model download handling now recover install commands, support setup-based downloads, and keep migrated custom presets deletable and launchable without stale checkout paths.
• 🐞 Update monitoring, tab restore, scroll restore, and panel status refresh now survive control restarts and long update flows more reliably.

v0.9.38

• 🖥️ Benchmarks and score UI were refined with focused score-chip styling, selectable running-preset logs, richer collapsed monitor telemetry, persisted monitor position, and better live stage labeling.
• ⚙️ Ineligible presets now stay visibly unscored in their own queue group with explicit reasons and cannot be benchmarked until missing hardware or model requirements are fixed.
• ⚙️ Full scoring now separates non-reasoning, reasoning, and optional sandbox quality lanes, with dedicated reasoning-only and sandbox-only rerun paths.
• ⚙️ Partial stage reruns now preserve the correct live runtime context, clear inherited artifacts and stale step results, and track explicit stage IDs so repair runs cannot masquerade as clean complete scores.
• ⚙️ Benchmark power and thermal handling was hardened with a 220W Benchmark Ready profile, direct Fast and Turbo power passes, reliable SIGCONT resume after cooldown, and benchmark-safe lower-power reruns for overheated presets.
• 🧰 Model Scores benchmarks now run through a dedicated worker service so active scoring survives control-service updates and retains ownership of benchmark power and fan controls.
• 🖥️ Detailed Preset Scores and the compact benchmark monitor now stay visually in sync with reruns, refreshes, live progress bars, and saved score re-derivations.
• 🔄 Manual and web-triggered updates now enter the same live Update Logs mode while preserving active benchmark workers instead of forcing a benchmark shutdown.

v0.9.37

• 🟢 Added Model Scores with Quick and Full benchmark orchestration, resumable per-preset result history, saved score snapshots, and durable benchmark queue state.
• 🖥️ Added preset-card score pills and a Detailed Preset Scores experience with radar views, metric breakdowns, Quick or Full switching, comparison overlays, and live rerun refreshes.
• 🧰 Added a full Benchmarks control surface with Benchmark All and Benchmark Preset flows, queue history, live per-preset logs, retry actions, and benchmark telemetry surfaced in Generation Stats.
• ⚡ Benchmarks can now fan out across available GPU scopes in parallel while still respecting runtime locks, GPU assignment, and per-preset cleanup between runs.
• ⚙️ Benchmarks gained real thermal and power management with Benchmark Ready preparation, cooldown pauses, max-fan handling, safe Turbo behavior, and scoring paths that tolerate heat-limited runs.
• ⚙️ Benchmark sessions can now cancel, resume, and recover more cleanly, preserving queue state across interruptions and recovering orphaned or partially finished rows instead of silently resetting them.
• 🖥️ The Benchmarks modal and compact monitor became persistent control centers with grouped queue states, focused logs, passive running rows, mobile-aware layout, and stable locked controls during live refreshes.
• 🧰 Added a Run Scripts browser that discovers upstream shell scripts, exposes per-script options and docs, and keeps execution logs inside the panel.
• 🧰 Benchmark execution now uses runtime locks, readiness checks, score snapshots keyed to canonical selectors, and direct-runtime compliance harnesses with best-of attempts and saved result JSON.
• 🖥️ Preset cards and Detailed Preset Scores now distinguish Quick and Full scores, preserve failure markers and capped results, and expose retry actions directly from the score surfaces.
• ⚡ Benchmark activity now publishes per-instance progress, throughput, and GPU assignment into the existing panel telemetry instead of living as a hidden background process.
• ⚙️ Thermal protection now includes fan boosting, cooldown windows, stage pausing, and throughput-specific handling so speed tests can run aggressively without turning routine heat limits into permanent score failures.
• 🧰 Benchmark state persistence was hardened so multi-GPU runs serialize worker writes safely and resume with queue, stage, and progress context intact after interruptions.
• 🔄 Updates and enabled-instance autoboot now coordinate with benchmark ownership so updates can respect active benchmarking state and restored runtimes no longer steal GPUs mid-run.

v0.9.36

• 🖥️ Refined preset-card, header-pill, storage-control, and Previous Folder UI details so the media and file surfaces read more cleanly on both desktop and mobile.
• 🟢 Multimedia previews now use authenticated server-served media paths, making image, audio, video, and PDF embeds more reliable while restoring seekable playback support.
• ⚙️ File Editor media playback gained subtitle discovery, embedded subtitle extraction, native-track versus overlay modes, and fullscreen-safe subtitle size and delay controls.
• 🖥️ Storage mount and unmount controls were visually rebuilt around clearer USB-style icons while subtitle-mode toggles preserve in-place video playback instead of restarting the file.

v0.9.35

• 🟢 Detached Metrics storage browsing matured into a practical multi-file workspace with minimized editors, an open-file taskbar, unsaved-close handling, and stronger popup state persistence across detach or reattach flows.
• 🧰 Storage browser downloads now support large-folder archive planning, temp-space validation, background zip creation, and progress polling so big exports no longer block the panel.
• 🖥️ Storage and editor UX was polished with clearer modal controls, steadier tab and button layout, persistent Volumes activity feedback, and broader binary-media preview support.

v0.9.34

• 🧰 Reworked the storage browser and text or hex editors around chunked 1 MB file sessions with a 1 GB open limit, giving the panel a scalable path for larger files without freezing the UI.
• 🟢 Detached Metrics browsers gained a Duplicate action for side-by-side folder browsing between popup and main window.
• 🐞 Detached storage browsers and editors now maintain independent popup versus main-window state, while small files still reopen through the simpler direct editor path.

v0.9.33

• 🖥️ Detached Metrics popup browsing was completed so the file navigator, file editor, and hex editor now stay inside the popup window instead of snapping back into the main panel.

v0.9.32

• 🛠️ Updated the build and self-update pipeline for the new `src`-based layout and root `metadata.json`, including SHA-aware GitHub metadata lookups and the new explicit `--iterative` release flow.
• 🟢 Added detachable Metrics popout and reattach behavior that mirrors the Logs workflow, including popup-local tab switching, confirmations, forwarded modal actions, and a clear in-panel detached placeholder.
• 🖥️ Detached Metrics polish continued with popup-local styling, live metric forwarding, detached storage and editor modal stacks, cleaner placeholders, and more reliable popup redraws and chart resizing.
• 🖥️ Mobile preset cards were tightened so Disk and TPS pills wrap more naturally while launch controls stay aligned.

v0.9.31

• 🟢 Added hidden-preset management with immediate reconciliation so hiding or restoring a preset updates the visible catalog instantly and survives later status polls.
• 🟢 Added a full model and cache management layer with a disk-usage overview, per-resource preset counts, shared resource markers, root filesystem browsing, and delete flows for models, caches, or both together.
• 🖥️ Expanded the storage browser and editors with asynchronous root-folder size estimation, cleaner file tables and toolbars, a responsive hex editor, and direct main-filesystem volume management from the panel.
• ⚙️ Preset resource markers were redesigned around a stable broader color palette so shared resources are easier to track across cards and cleanup dialogs.
• 🛠️ Release metadata moved fully into GitHub `metadata.json`, and the versioning/build pipeline gained correct letter-suffix support plus the new root-layout update path.
• 🧩 Compatibility handling was updated for legacy DFlash vLLM presets and the migrated upstream target so supported installs no longer show false unsupported-version warnings.

v0.9.30

• 🟢 Added optional GDDR6 or GDDR6X junction and VRAM temperature telemetry to GPU cards and Metrics charts, with the helper source and NVML header bundled directly into the installer.
• 🧰 Install, update, and migrate flows now provision the helper dependencies, stage the needed kernel setting, and warn about reboots only when the running kernel still needs it.
• ⚙️ Added a `--skip-temps` installer switch for operators who want to keep the previous lighter install behavior without the extra telemetry setup.

v0.9.29

• ⚡ Reduced the shipped admin and control payload further by trimming unreferenced helper paths while preserving runtime behavior.
• 🐞 Restored Generation Stats KV, context, prefix, and token label formatting expected by the panel.

v0.9.28

• 🐞 Restored the Metrics GPU chart rendering path so temperature, power, utilization, and VRAM charts display correctly again while keeping the faster metrics polling behavior.

v0.9.27

• 🐞 Fixed Metrics cold-start loading so chart history is warmed at startup and GPU charts render current cached telemetry immediately instead of opening blank.

v0.9.26

• 🐞 Streamed markdown list rendering now repairs split ordered and unordered markers during generation, so technical replies keep proper list structure without waiting for final render.

v0.9.25

• 🐞 Chat token accounting now reports true per-message input usage instead of whole prompt context, and the narrowed live plain-preview fallback preserves rich markdown finalization for dollar-heavy or code-heavy replies.

v0.9.24

• ⚡ Compressed payload declarations were compacted onto single-line base64 strings, shaving more size from the installer without changing behavior.

v0.9.23

• ⚡ Reduced installer size again by max-gzip packing the embedded control and updater Python payloads behind small runtime unpackers.

v0.9.22

• ⚡ Reduced shipped installer size by compressing the embedded admin web UI payload and enabling stronger safe JavaScript minification.
• ⚡ Removed dead migrated UI leftovers from the admin bundle so only the current panel code ships.

v0.9.21

• 🧰 Model-install requests now accept canonical preset install commands more flexibly and recover download-size reporting better, so built-in Hugging Face and setup-driven downloads surface meaningful progress again.
• ⚙️ Chat persistence and hydration were hardened so large markdown-heavy conversations save reliably, auto-scroll preference persists, conversation titles finish loading before the UI unlocks, and saved `stream-state` can self-heal missed final replies.
• ⚙️ Scientific and math-heavy chat rendering now avoids the old streaming freezes by using safer markdown fallbacks and better ordered-list detection, while still preserving full markdown once a reply is complete.
• 🖥️ Generation Statistics regained cumulative conversation totals, per-message token labels, live used-versus-total context display, and KV usage for newer llama.cpp-style runtime logs.
• 🛠️ Build releases now require explicit `--change` notes and automatically update `metadata.json` plus the changelog before packaging.

v0.9.20

• 🖥️ Metrics now preserve true persistent maxima across restarts and add a trash-can reset action for clearing saved history and peaks from the normal confirmation flow.
• ⚡ Reduced hidden-tab Metrics and syntax-highlight work during long chats so repeated long generations stop freezing the web UI.

v0.9.19

• 🧰 Preset install jobs now consistently route through the built-in admin downloader first, even for setup-driven installs, restoring uniform Audit Log progress and cleaner model-install planning.
• 🐞 The updater Python resolution path was hardened so `--update` no longer derails on polluted `PYTHON_BIN` values before rewriting managed files.
• 🐞 Preset inventory and launch validation now preserve required model, draft-model, and projector paths correctly, keeping lanes like `beellama/dflash` launchable and diagnosable.
• 🧩 The live `beellama/dflash` rollout and related inventory warnings were cleaned up so shipped assets, runtime image requirements, and fallback recipe probes stay aligned.

v0.9.18

• 🧰 Preset install jobs were normalized around the built-in admin downloader, restoring progress visibility and cleaner setup handoff for Hugging Face-backed installs.
• 🐞 Inventory and launch validation now preserve required model, draft-model, and projector paths so presets like `beellama/dflash` remain launchable after compaction.
• 🧩 The split-source model-install helper and live `beellama/dflash` rollout were aligned so rebuilt admins no longer depend on the monolith for correct download and launch behavior.

v0.9.17

• 🧰 Preset install jobs were reworked so Hugging Face downloads flow through the admin downloader with visible progress, while split-source model-install helpers and `beellama/dflash` rollout stay aligned.

v0.9.16

• 🧰 Preset install jobs were reworked so Hugging Face and setup-driven downloads use the admin downloader path with visible progress, and the `beellama/dflash` lane was validated end-to-end on the live host.

v0.9.15

• 🐞 Restored split-source live Generation Stats updates during active chat generations, including correct `conversation_id` propagation and speculative or prompt-cache stats for ik-llama runtimes.

v0.9.14

• 🐞 The GitHub update checker now tolerates oversized remote changelogs, restoring green update detection without forced updates.
• 🧰 Preset downloads regained live Audit Log progress across both direct Hugging Face pulls and setup-driven installs.
• 🖥️ Preset cards were polished with correct Stop-state rendering, source tooltips on Download buttons, and restored NVLink grouping for newer upstream compose metadata.

v0.9.13

• 🐞 Main-tab Generation Statistics and speculative or MTP stats now refresh live during active generations again, with fast status polling restored and missing prefix-cache reads normalized cleanly.

v0.9.12

• 🖥️ The Presets Summary selector was stabilized so Summary reliably returns to the cached overview instead of snapping back to a prior model tab.
• 🖥️ Preset disk markers were cleaned up so GGUF or model assets, projector files, download-cache placeholders, and directory-backed resources read more clearly at a glance.
• 🐞 Live Generation Statistics again apply incremental stream metrics during active chats, including speculative or MTP fields.

v0.9.11

• 🖥️ The Presets Summary selector, preset disk markers, and live speculative Generation Statistics were all restored to the intended v0.9 behavior after the earlier regression.

v0.9.10

• 🖥️ Added per-resource disk markers beside each preset Disk label, with shared resources reusing colors across presets and projector sidecars rendering distinctly as hollow markers.
• 🖥️ Reworked preset status and download badge colors so deprecated, caveat, blocked, needs-download, exited, and production states are easier to distinguish.
• 🐞 Disk accounting now treats Hugging Face cache-backed and SGLang presets more honestly, and streamed markdown open-code previews preserve line breaks while a fence is still incomplete.

v0.9.9

• 🖥️ Restored the scoped Benchmark, Run Report, and Rebench actions to the correct cards after the migrated layout regressed, and removed the dead layout shim carrying the older button arrangement.

v0.9.8

• ⚙️ Live-retuned the new Qwen 3.6 APEX and Hauhau presets for RTX 3090-class hardware, documenting the fastest stable KV and MTP defaults and promoting Luffy Genesis to the validated 196K profile.

v0.9.7

• 🖥️ Restored the richer v0.8-era admin UX that the early v0.9 split had lost, including the Debug Logs bash lane, detached runtime log selector, System card actions, Setup Assistant survey, advanced preset grouping, and saved-chat load-original-preset flow.
• 🟢 Added per-preset disk-usage labels and an explicit Delete flow that shows the exact downloaded resources and reclaim size before removing them.
• 🟢 Added a per-GPU free-resources trash action on Main GPU cards so operators can stop stray runtime containers and reclaim VRAM even for containers started outside the panel.
• ⚙️ Runtime launches plus proxy or local inference now wake GPUs back into the Default power profile before doing work.
• 🐞 Multi-download and custom-model job status handling was restored so parallel model installs remain visible in the Presets tab.

v0.9.6

• 🖥️ Restored the v0.8-era Debug Logs shell lane, detached runtime log selector, and System card actions after the v0.9 compose split regressions.
• 🟢 Added per-preset disk-usage labels, explicit model-delete flows, and a per-GPU free-resources action for reclaiming VRAM from orphaned runtime containers.
• ⚙️ Runtime launches and inference requests now wake GPUs back into the Default power profile before starting work.
• 🧩 Marked the migrated upstream Club-3090 target and its dirty or local describe variants as compatible so supported installs no longer show false unsupported warnings.

v0.9.5

• 🟢 Added experimental built-in Qwen 3.6 APEX-MTP and HauhauCS preset families with direct Hugging Face downloads and a shared thinking-aware Jinja chat template.
• ⚡ Retuned the APEX fit presets around the validated q8_0 K and q4_0 V hybrid so single-card RTX 3090 systems keep 196K context with better throughput.
• 🖥️ Added persistent Max and Avg TPS labels to preset cards, backed by per-preset inference history with direct reset gestures.
• 🖥️ Restored Preset Launch Settings so saved context, model, and launch overrides remain aligned after the v0.9 compose migration.
• 🧩 Runtime inventory, migration setup, and preset switching were updated for the new upstream compose hierarchy so GGUF downloads, migration replay, and preset changes stop pinning stale VRAM or aborting after successful pulls.

v0.9.4

• 🧩 Restored canonical preset selectors on rebuilt runtime-inventory variants so the v0.9 compose registry stays aligned with selector-driven launch settings, cached preset state, and custom tooling.

v0.9.3

• 🐞 Fixed relative `MODEL_DIR` resolution and normalized custom-model path ownership checks so compose-relative models and imported preset folders resolve and clean up correctly during installs, migrations, and launches.

v0.9.2

• 🐞 Fixed v0.9 nested compose-family launch paths and imported custom-model fallback paths so per-instance boots, global vLLM switches, inventory rebuilds, and migrations all resolve the correct cache, patch, and compose roots again.

v0.9.1

• 🐞 Restored the split runtime-inventory rebuild path and default single or dual selector helpers so standalone rebuilds, migrations, and update checks can reconstruct the v0.9 preset catalog correctly.

v0.9.0

• 🧩 Updated Club-3090 Server for the reworked upstream compose hierarchy, so nested preset families across vLLM, llama.cpp, ik-llama, and newer Qwen or Gemma variants are discovered and launched correctly again.
• 🧰 Replaced stale preset download heuristics with profile-driven asset planning from current upstream metadata, improving Download versus Ready state handling for newer preset families.
• 🧩 Reworked `--migrate` to replay the needed upstream setup commands on the fresh checkout and normalize preserved GGUF assets into the new expected paths.
• 🖥️ Split `ik-llama` back out from the generic `llama.cpp` label in preset cards so the UI reflects the actual runtime family again.

v0.8.47

• 🟢 Debug Logs transfers can now expand typed folder paths or globs into zip downloads from the current shell context, and shift-click or long-press uploads can send whole folders back the same way with streamed progress.
• 🖥️ The Debug Logs command box also got saner editing, draft recovery, and command-history deduplication behavior.

v0.8.45

• 🟢 Debug Logs transfers can now expand typed folder paths or globs into zip downloads from the current shell context, and shift-click or long-press uploads can send whole folders back with streamed progress.
• 🖥️ The Debug Logs command box regained normal editing, draft recovery, and deduplicated history handling.

v0.8.44

• 🐞 Preset Launch Settings runtime stats now reflect the actual launched context overrides instead of always falling back to compose defaults, and bundled preset sources were refreshed for the newer live-host PRISM-PRO-DQ and APEX families.

v0.8.43

• 🖥️ Admin modals no longer dismiss on backdrop clicks, making text selection and other in-modal work stable until an explicit close or action button is used.
• 🖥️ Debug Logs autocomplete evolved into a fish-style shell lane with inline ghost completion, color-coded history or path suggestions, `Ctrl+Space` completion, rolling history, and detached-popout runtime selection that stays synchronized.
• 🖥️ Custom Model and Preset Launch Settings now expose a custom engine-switches textarea and cleaner structured fields so launch overrides are easier to inspect and edit.
• ⚙️ Thinking-aware APEX and proxy or admin chat behavior was aligned so no-thinking requests stop leaking leading think blocks while reasoning-enabled requests still get the right template and token handling.
• ⚙️ Saved chat conversations now preserve their original preset and container placement more faithfully, including unavailable presets and pending send-after-boot flows.
• 🧰 Direct-file Hugging Face downloads regained 5% Audit Log progress updates, and shared mmproj assets are normalized into hardlinks instead of duplicated projector files.

v0.8.42

• 🧰 Extended the GPU remap fix across the broader launch surface by exporting host GPU IDs separately from container-local CUDA ordinals, protecting higher-index single, dual, and imported preset boots.
• 🧪 Added regression coverage so future GPU1+ launch regressions fail the build before they silently ship.

v0.8.41

• 🐞 Fixed Global-scope ik-llama single-card fan-out and generated single or dual override files so higher-index GPU launches keep the correct physical reservation while still seeing the right container-local CUDA ordinals.

v0.8.40

• 🐞 Fixed llama.cpp and ik-llama boot completion detection so non-vLLM single-card presets clear Booting as soon as their real runtime API is reachable and report live readiness correctly.

v0.8.39

• 🟢 Added a launch-settings gear to preset cards with selector-scoped persistent overrides for compose-exposed launch envs plus advanced manual env overrides.
• 🐞 Newer llama.cpp and ik-llama single-card presets now trust their live compose defaults for context sizing and promoted-status labeling, restoring sensible max-context reporting.
• 🧰 Simple Hugging Face preset downloads regained visible 5% Audit Log progress, and supported-upstream metadata moved forward to the newer live target.

v0.8.38

• ⚡ Fixed cold-start first-token stalls on affected vLLM presets by forcing a persistent absolute Triton or vLLM compile-cache path and paying the initial warmup generation cost during startup instead of on the first user prompt.

v0.8.37

• 🐞 Fixed the ik-llama Qwen 3.6 preset download wiring and compose-path parsing so those presets request the right GGUF and mmproj assets directly instead of leaking generic setup paths or unresolved placeholders into the UI.

v0.8.36

• 🟢 Reworked the Debug Logs shell lane into a broader live-shell workspace with stacked upload/download controls, shell-aware file transfers, sudo-backed reads and writes, multi-file zip downloads, and streamed progress in the debug viewer.
• 🧩 Supported-upstream metadata moved to the migrated upstream checkout, and newer Qwen 3.6 ik-llama presets now surface missing assets and startup failures more honestly.

v0.8.35

• 🐞 Extended the live chat markdown split guard so streamed replies no longer sever common multi-line inline formatting spans like bold, emphasis, code, strikethrough, and links when paragraphs wrap.
• 🧪 Added regression coverage so those multi-line streaming markdown cases fail the build if they regress again.

v0.8.34

• ⚙️ Chat state saving was reworked so long conversations serialize pending writes instead of aborting them, large active chats stop failing to save, and runtime-stat churn stays local where it can.
• 🐞 Added orphaned-stream recovery so reloads or network hiccups can clear dead generating sessions and let the conversation continue cleanly.

v0.8.33

• 🟢 Added backend-backed per-conversation stop control for live streams, allowing recovered or refreshed chats to send a real abort to the server.
• 🐞 Ordered-list markdown continuation rendering was also repaired, and smoke coverage was added for both the recovered-stop path and the list regression.

v0.8.32

• ⚡ Smoothed live transcript follow again by increasing stream cadence, softening the scroll easing, and expanding temporary growth reserve so long replies stay visible with less jumpiness.

v0.8.31

• ⚡ Further tuned live chat auto-follow with gentler scroll steps, a smaller follow band, and a final completion settle to the true bottom, while stopping ordinary conversation switches from auto-scrolling the transcript.

v0.8.30

• ⚡ Reworked live chat auto-follow around a softer target zone that keeps the newest rendered line around the lower part of the viewport instead of jamming content against the bottom edge.
• 🐞 The follow loop and streaming markdown boundaries were also repaired so auto-scroll does not drop out mid-generation and numbered-list fragments do not split while content is still arriving.

v0.8.29

• ⚡ Refined the new transcript follow loop so it can self-correct both downward and upward while keeping the newest line anchored near the intended reserve band.

v0.8.28

• ⚡ Added a more resilient transcript-follow fallback that uses `scrollIntoView` where available and automatic bottom-pinning where it is not, keeping both the shipped panel and browser-smoke harness stable.

v0.8.27

• ⚡ Reworked transcript follow mode around a persistent bottom sentinel plus RAF-scheduled `scrollIntoView`, preserving follow behavior through full rerenders and placeholder states during active generation.

v0.8.26

• ⚡ Reworked chat follow mode to use a CSS bottom anchor instead of repeated `scrollTop` writes and replaced the composer height-sync loop with CSS-native textarea growth, smoothing both transcript and input resizing during active chats.

v0.8.25

• 🐞 Reworked the transcript resize-handle gesture so fit-to-content works more reliably on desktop and mobile, and continued expanding `web/code_syntax.json` into an explicit per-family source of truth for token categories.

v0.8.24

• 🐞 Fixed the transcript resize-handle triple-click path with a more reliable pointer-up tap counter and normalized `web/code_syntax.json` so every language family explicitly exposes the full supported token schema.

v0.8.23

• ⚡ Smoothed chat streaming while auto-scroll is enabled by stopping follow scrolls from retriggering the interaction defer path, so replies keep rendering token-by-token instead of bunching into visible batches.
• 🟢 Added a persistent chat transcript auto-scroll checkbox beneath the transcript with the same style as the log-viewer toggle.
• 🖥️ Removed the transcript resize cap, added triple-click fit-to-content on the resize handle, and expanded syntax theme coverage so more code token categories use dedicated configured colors.

v0.8.22

• 🐞 Fixed the standalone `web-ui.test.html` artifact so it reports the current build script version instead of a stale fallback.

v0.8.21

• ⚡ Smoothed live chat streaming by locking transcript height during active generation and batching bottom-follow work, cutting the resize and reflow churn behind long-reply stutter.
• 🧰 Removed the old code-syntax fallback path so the panel now treats the rewritten local `code_syntax.json` as its single source of truth and fully overwrites it on startup.
• 🐞 Reworked generation-stat latency to represent request-to-prompt-start timing more honestly instead of whole-turn elapsed time.

v0.8.20

• ⚡ Reworked live chat transcript streaming so open markdown stays on a lightweight plain-text lane during generation and snaps back through the full markdown renderer once blocks or the reply finish.
• 🐞 Code-syntax refreshes now hard-bust browser and admin caches more aggressively and rehighlight existing code blocks from preserved raw source when token rules or themes change.
• 🐞 Generation-stat latency reporting was corrected so TTFT stays live during generation without publishing a fake ever-increasing latency number.

v0.8.19

• ⚡ Continued smoothing live transcript streaming and code-syntax refresh behavior for active chats so long replies stay more responsive while syntax updates propagate more reliably.

v0.8.18

• ⚡ Continued the chat streaming and syntax-refresh stabilization work for long replies, keeping live transcript updates lighter and more reliable during generation.

v0.8.17

• ⚡ Continued the newer live chat streaming path with lighter in-generation rendering, improved code-syntax refresh behavior, and corrected TTFT-style latency reporting for active turns.

v0.8.16

• 🐞 Restored the green update banner and button quieting while the Run Update modal is open, improved same-page and refresh-based live stream recovery, and made code-syntax refreshes more authoritative when shipped themes change.
• 🖥️ Chat and runtime stat labels were also cleaned up for more consistent punctuation and per-message wording.

v0.8.15

• 🐞 Update polling now stays quiet while the Run Update modal is open, live chat stream recovery is steadier across refreshes, and shipped code-syntax refreshes now overwrite stale on-disk data more aggressively.
• 🖥️ Chat and runtime stat labels were normalized for clearer per-message output, TTFT, and tk/s display.

v0.8.14

• ⚙️ Chat-message persistence was hardened so per-message token and speed stats, conversation totals, generation flags, and in-flight stream recovery all survive reloads instead of being stripped out on resave.
• 🖥️ Mobile transcript rendering pressure was reduced while the user is scrolling or resizing during active generations.
• 🐞 Runtime telemetry now distinguishes true per-message counts from whole-request context totals and includes live KV usage during streaming.

v0.8.13

• 🖥️ Finished a large markdown and markup rendering cleanup pass, restoring visible HTML/XML coloring, transcript footnote and relative-anchor behavior, Mermaid export icons, and type-matched admonition accents.
• 🐞 Chat and runtime telemetry handling was corrected so per-message token badges, cumulative conversation stats, generating states, and KV or prefix reporting stay consistent.
• 🟢 The Debug Log viewer gained an interactive bash command lane, and long-running install or log streams now flush carriage-return progress updates into Audit Logs instead of stalling early.
• 🖥️ Model-family cards now generate family-level descriptions instead of repeating the first preset summary.

v0.8.12

• 🖥️ Restored visible full-markup syntax coloring for HTML or XML code blocks and tuned the tag versus attribute palette for clearer separation without changing the overall theme direction.

v0.8.11

• 🛠️ Reworked the markup syntax highlighter to tokenize raw HTML or XML before the final safe-escape pass, fixing the double-escaping issue so literal `<` and `>` display correctly again.

v0.8.10

• 🧩 Experimental runtime variants now stay classified as Experimental all the way from backend inventory generation through preset UI grouping.
• 🧰 Expanded the lightweight built-in LaTeX renderer toward a broader KaTeX-style baseline with more symbol, matrix, ellipsis, and row-break support.

v0.8.9

• 🛠️ Removed the `--allow-stale-metadata` bypass so `build.py` always enforces metadata freshness during live builds.
• 🖥️ The Setup Assistant survey was refreshed and renamed to Preset Recommendation Survey, with better experimental grouping and clearer Custom Model sidebar presentation.

v0.8.8

• 🐞 Installer and migration flows now leave the upstream checkout clean before handing off to `scripts/update.sh`, avoiding dirty-working-tree failures on fresh installs.
• ⚡ Trimmed genuinely dead helper paths out of the split sources without changing live behavior.

v0.8.7

• 🐞 Fixed the split-source web build mismatch so the real `web/` sources, shipped assets, and generated installer all carry the same Summary-tab, preview-grouping, advanced-preset, blocked-launch, and Setup Assistant changes.
• 🛠️ `build.py` now removes generated root bundle files by default after successful builds, with `--keep-files` as the opt-out for artifact inspection.

v0.8.6

• ⚡ Global single and dual launches now fan out across eligible GPUs or pairs in parallel instead of waiting for each container to become ready before starting the next one.
• 🟢 Model-download tracking became a real multi-job system, letting multiple installs run concurrently with visible status instead of one request freezing the others.
• 🧩 Added a local Hugging Face fallback chain for the Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B AutoRound downloads so the stale upstream repo slug no longer blocks installs.
• 🖥️ The model and setup UI was refined with corrected summary selection, clearer NVLink and multi-GPU grouping, better preview or experimental badge handling, a blocked-launch warning modal, and a refreshed Setup Assistant custom-import lane.

v0.8.5

• 🐞 Self-update and migrate flows now read freshly written server and service state before readiness waits and preserve existing HTTPS mode correctly, preventing multi-minute stalls or silent downgrades back to plain HTTP.

v0.8.4

• 🐞 Self-update completion was hardened so controller-only updates stop waiting on absent Caddy services when HTTPS is off and read fresh service state before readiness waits.

v0.8.3

• 🛠️ Reworked remote update metadata so the installer, updater, and panel all resolve changelog and release data from the same SHA-pinned `build/metadata.json` source instead of parsing release notes out of the installer script.
• 🖥️ The Run Update modal now renders both changelog sections strictly from fetched GitHub metadata, and the old embedded release-history shell constant was removed from the installer contract.

v0.8.1

• 🟢 Rebuilt the Setup Assistant into a real recommendation quiz that maps workload answers back into model-first preset suggestions and a suggested custom-import reference profile.
• 🖥️ Reworked the Custom Model entry point with a cleaner two-column modal, new plus-icon treatment, and clearer safety and override guidance around the detected rig context.
• 📝 Corrected `REMOTE.md` so remote audits and tunnels target the live server’s real internal admin listener.

v0.8.0

• 🧩 Migrated the preset inventory to the upstream v0.8 registry and profile model so the panel discovers the modern catalog, including newer Qwen and Gemma families, shared-compose aliases, and richer install metadata.
• 🟢 Added a first-class Custom Model flow in Presets, including a `[+] Custom Model` entry point, streamed upstream import logs, persistent custom runtime cards, and proper custom-model teardown.
• 🖥️ Reworked the Setup Assistant around the newer upstream recommendation model with rig-aware guidance, clearer production or preview cues, and an explicit advanced path for upstream custom imports.
• 🧰 Preserved the live host’s Hugging Face CLI improvements, installed the missing `python-yaml` dependency for v0.8 profiles, and repaired the live admin white-page outage from the accidental placeholder backend deployment.
• 🐞 Launch-path regressions uncovered on the live host were corrected so custom runtimes keep their own model path, registry-backed rebuilds classify newer AWQ and MoE variants correctly, and vLLM compose files regain the correct nightly pin.

v0.7.12

• 🖥️ Users-tab and custom API preset save/delete flows now reconcile the visible directory and preset cards immediately instead of waiting for a later reload.
• 🖥️ Auxiliary-service status cards now present clearer terminal-state and exit-code information.
• 🛠️ Changelog metadata handling was hardened by embedding `CHANGE_LOG_RELEASE`, sanitizing parsed control characters, and fixing the remote update fetch helper’s broken curl return path.

v0.7.11

• ⚡ Restored incremental syntax highlighting timing for streamed replies so fenced code blocks pick up coloring as soon as each block is finalized.

v0.7.10

• ⚡ Streaming markdown now keeps inline single-backtick code rendering correctly during generation and forces the final fenced-code syntax pass as soon as a reply completes.

v0.7.9

• 🐞 Audit and debug logging were cleaned up so repeated auth-denied probes and verbose admin result dumps stop polluting the human-facing Audit view while still remaining available in `debug.log`.
• ⚡ Streaming markdown kept improving with correct inline single-backtick rendering during generation and immediate final fenced-code highlighting.
• 🖥️ The Docker log instance selector for Global launches now expands into concrete runtime targets like `GPU0 (Global)` and `PAIR0_1 (Global)` instead of a misleading single scope.

v0.7.8

• ⚡ Packed `code_syntax.json` into the shipped control payload as gzip plus base64, unpacked it on startup, and served it from `/admin/code-syntax` so the large syntax blob no longer lives inside shipped JavaScript.
• 🛠️ Consolidated retained v0.7 release history into a single `v0.7.0` section and trimmed more dead chat-side control code without changing runtime behavior.

v0.7.0

• 🧰 Split the backend monolith into the new `control/` source tree, rebuilt the canonical target model around `GLOBAL`, `GPU<n>`, and `PAIRx_y`, removed the legacy dual-runtime path, and made sequential auto-pairs the built-in dual layout while preserving custom pairs.
• 🖥️ Split the admin frontend into the new `web/` source tree, removed the old GPU-pairing toggle, updated scope and pair handling around automatic sequential pairs, and blocked deletion of built-in auto-pairs while keeping custom pair management.
• 🛠️ Reworked the build pipeline around `build/`, `web/`, and `control/`, extracted the smoke harness, added the root `build.py` wrapper, moved build logs to `logs/`, and regenerated the root monolith artifacts from the split sources.
• ⚡ Deleted more dead legacy branches, inert compatibility leftovers, and unreferenced UI helpers so the shipped installer and JavaScript shrink without changing current runtime behavior.
• 🖥️ Reworked the Instances scope strip, modal close controls, GPU-picker flows, and Wake-on-LAN modal so the refactored UI feels more intentional and consistent.
• ⚙️ Dual-launch behavior and stop handoffs were tuned around the new model so default pair preference, compose launch roots, and replacement-runtime shutdown all behave correctly under the auto-pair system.
• 🔄 Self-update source normalization and updater execution were hardened so downloaded installer copies, canonical GitHub feed settings, and readiness probes stay aligned through updates.
• 📝 The design docs, migration plan, and progress-tracking docs were rewritten around the refactored architecture so the v0.7 and v0.8 tracks can be resumed or audited cleanly later.
